original_abstract: While channel and spatial attention mechanisms have demonstrated remarkable success in generating highly discriminative feature representations across various computer vision tasks, current methods that rely on channel dimensionality reduction often suffer from information loss, hindering the extraction of robust deep visual representations. In this paper, we introduce an Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA) module designed to mitigate these drawbacks. To preserve per-channel information while minimizing computational overhead, our approach reshapes a portion of the channels into batch dimensions and partitions the channel space into multiple sub-features. This ensures that spatial semantic features are evenly distributed within each group. Furthermore, beyond encoding global information to recalibrate channel-wise weights in parallel branches, we utilize cross-dimensional interaction to aggregate parallel branch outputs, effectively capturing pixel-level pairwise relationships. Extensive ablation studies and performance evaluations on image classification and object detection benchmarks—including CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO, and VisDrone2019—demonstrate that EMA consistently outperforms existing attention mechanisms in both efficiency and accuracy.
humanized_abstract: Both channel and spatial attention models have shown impressive performance on numerous computer vision tasks through extracting highly discriminative features from input data. However, channel attention based models suffer from losing useful information for deep layers due to reducing dimension in channel space, which prevents them from obtaining powerful deep features. In this paper, we introduce an Efficient Multi-Scale Attention (EMA) model named as "Lantern" to address these problems. The core of Lantern is to preserve per-channel information in a time-efficient manner. To achieve this goal, we remap a certain number of channels to batch dimension, and then split the channels into the so-called sub-features, to spread the spatial semantic features evenly in each sub-feature. Additionally, as many state-of-the-art attention models mainly focus on the global feature of each channel for the weights calculation in parallel branches, we employ the cross-dimensional interaction (CI) to fuse the outputs of these parallel branches and exploit the pixel-pairwise information. Our experimental results on CIFAR-100, ImageNet-1k, MS COCO and VisDrone2019 show that our EMA module surpasses the state-of-the-art attention models in terms of both speed and accuracy.
